mood.label: CAUTIOUSLY-RISK-ON
mood.summary: Futures are pointing to a strong rebound after yesterday's Fed-induced selloff, but the tape remains reactive. Easing geopolitical tensions and falling oil prices are supportive, but leadership is narrow.

indices.name: 10-Yr Treasury Yield
indices.note: Yields remain firm after hawkish Fed comments.

indices.name: Crude Oil (WTI)
indices.note: Prices falling on US-Iran deal and reopening of Strait of Hormuz.

indicesNarrative: U.S. indices sold off sharply yesterday after the Fed adopted a more hawkish tone, with nearly half of policymakers seeing a rate hike in 2026. However, futures are indicating a strong rebound this morning, fueled by an easing of geopolitical tensions after a U.S.-Iran peace deal was signed, which is also pushing oil prices lower. The Nasdaq is set to lead the bounce.
technicalsNarrative: Based on yesterday's close, SPY's technicals look weak, with an RSI of 35.8 and most moving averages flashing 'Sell' signals after the sharp drop. This suggests the index was approaching oversold territory, setting up for today's pre-market bounce. With futures pointing to a strong open, the key will be whether indices can reclaim key moving averages and hold the gains.

sectorsThemes.name: Technology
sectorsThemes.note: Remains the clear market leader, driven by AI enthusiasm.

sectorsThemes.name: Energy
sectorsThemes.note: Under pressure as crude oil prices fall on geopolitical developments.

sectorsThemes.name: Utilities
sectorsThemes.note: Defensive sector that has been lagging in the recent risk-on environment.

sectorsNarrative: Market leadership remains extremely narrow, with Technology as the dominant outperformer. Defensive sectors like Utilities and Energy have been the primary laggards, a trend likely to continue with falling oil prices.
industryNarrative: Semiconductors and AI-related stocks continue to be the strongest industry groups, driving the gains in the Technology sector. Conversely, interest-rate sensitive groups and commodity-related industries have been underperforming.

Upgraded to buy from neutral at Citi on lithium price expectations.
Issued a disappointing Q4 revenue forecast below Wall Street expectations.
Significant pre-market decline on high volume, catalyst pending.
movers.traderLens: Breakdown, avoid catching a falling knife (Jeff).

watchlist.note: Watching for follow-through with the market bounce.
Up pre-market after CEO comments on raising prices.
Slightly down pre-market, watching for direction at the open.

Key name in the server/AI space.
Needs to prove itself against overhead resistance.

traderLens.name: Minervini
traderLens.view: Market is attempting a rebound after a pullback. Look for leaders to emerge, but don't chase extended names.

traderLens.name: Ted Zhang
traderLens.view: Rotation remains focused on Tech and AI. Money flows out of Energy and defensives.

traderLens.name: Clement Ang
traderLens.view: Market context is choppy (Whipsaw Wednesday). Wait for confirmation of the bounce before deploying significant capital. Grade is B-.

traderLens.name: SRxTrades
traderLens.view: Wait for clean pivots and volume confirmation on any long entries today. Avoid chasing the gap up open.

traderLens.name: Jeff
traderLens.view: Respect stops. Yesterday's reversal shows how quickly the tape can turn. Don't get caught on the wrong side.

traderLens.name: Qullamaggie
traderLens.view: Looking for high-momentum names that gap up and hold their gains, providing an opportunity for an entry.

traderLens.name: Composite
traderLens.view: Be patient at the open. The gap up is significant, especially in tech. The best trades may come from letting the market settle and finding names with relative strength that set up clean intraday pivots. Do not chase.

news.headline: US stocks eye rebound after Fed's hawkish tone sparked selloff
US and Iran sign interim peace deal, easing Mideast tensions and oil prices
Accenture tumbles 11% in premarket after weak Q4 revenue forecast
Kroger reports Q1 EPS beat, maintains 2026 guidance
